content_en:
The Jam' al-Jawami' became one of the most widely taught texts in usul al-fiqh throughout the Shafi'i world from the fifteenth century onward. Its adoption was facilitated by the prestige of the commentary associated with al-Mahalli and as-Suyuti, which provided the expansion and context needed to make the dense matan accessible to students. This matan-sharh (base text plus commentary) combination became the standard vehicle for teaching usul al-fiqh in Shafi'i madrasas from Cairo to Southeast Asia.

The influence of the Jam' al-Jawami' extended far beyond the Arab world. As Islam spread into Southeast Asia, Shafi'i scholarship traveled with it, and the Jam' al-Jawami' with as-Suyuti's commentary became a foundational text in pesantren (traditional Islamic boarding schools) in Java, in pondok schools throughout the Malay world, and in Islamic educational institutions from West Africa to Central Asia. The work's reach gives it a global significance that few other usul texts can match.

Numerous supercommentaries (hashiyah) were written on the commentary of as-Suyuti, further extending the pedagogical tradition attached to the text. These hashiyahs provided additional explanation, addressed objections, and related the discussions to contemporary legal questions, keeping the text actively relevant across generations. The layer upon layer of commentary attached to the Jam' al-Jawami' illustrates how the classical Islamic educational system transmitted learning through the vehicle of a single authoritative text.

Modern Islamic educational institutions in Egypt, the Levant, Southeast Asia, and elsewhere continue to teach the Jam' al-Jawami' with its commentaries as part of their usul al-fiqh curriculum. The work's conciseness makes it suitable for integration into curricula that also include other disciplines, while its comprehensiveness ensures that students who study it have been introduced to all the essential topics of the field.

Academic scholars have studied the Jam' al-Jawami' both as a text in its own right and as a window into the broader tradition it synthesizes. The work's role in the global diffusion of Shafi'i usul al-fiqh has attracted particular attention from scholars interested in the spread of Islamic learning through trade and educational networks. Studies of pesantren curriculum and Malay Islamic scholarship regularly discuss the Jam' al-Jawami' as a foundational text.
content_id:
Jam al-Jawami menjadi salah satu teks yang paling banyak diajarkan dalam usul fikih di seluruh dunia Syafi'i sejak abad kelima belas dan seterusnya. Penerimaannya difasilitasi oleh prestise syarah yang terkait dengan al-Mahalli dan as-Suyuti, yang menyediakan pengembangan dan konteks yang dibutuhkan untuk membuat matan yang padat itu dapat diakses oleh para pelajar. Kombinasi matan-syarah ini menjadi sarana standar pengajaran usul fikih di madrasah-madrasah Syafi'i dari Kairo hingga Asia Tenggara.

Pengaruh Jam al-Jawami meluas jauh melampaui dunia Arab. Seiring Islam menyebar ke Asia Tenggara, keilmuan Syafi'i turut bersamanya, dan Jam al-Jawami beserta syarah as-Suyuti menjadi teks fondasi di pesantren (sekolah berasrama Islam tradisional) di Jawa, di sekolah-sekolah pondok di seluruh dunia Melayu, serta di lembaga-lembaga pendidikan Islam dari Afrika Barat hingga Asia Tengah. Jangkauan karya ini memberikannya signifikansi global yang jarang dimiliki oleh teks-teks usul lainnya.

Banyak hasyiyah (superkomenter) ditulis atas syarah as-Suyuti, semakin memperluas tradisi pedagogis yang melekat pada teks ini. Hasyiyah-hasyiyah ini memberikan penjelasan tambahan, menangani keberatan-keberatan, dan mengaitkan diskusi-diskusi dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan hukum kontemporer, menjaga teks tetap relevan secara aktif dari generasi ke generasi. Lapisan demi lapisan syarah yang melekat pada Jam al-Jawami menggambarkan bagaimana sistem pendidikan Islam klasik meneruskan keilmuan melalui wahana sebuah teks tunggal yang otoritatif.

Lembaga-lembaga pendidikan Islam modern di Mesir, Levant, Asia Tenggara, dan tempat lain terus mengajarkan Jam al-Jawami beserta syarah-syarahnya sebagai bagian dari kurikulum usul fikih mereka. Keringkasan karya ini menjadikannya cocok untuk diintegrasikan ke dalam kurikulum yang juga mencakup disiplin-disiplin lain, sementara komprehensivitasnya memastikan bahwa para pelajar yang mempelajarinya telah diperkenalkan dengan semua topik esensial dalam bidang ini.

Para akademisi telah mempelajari Jam al-Jawami baik sebagai teks tersendiri maupun sebagai jendela ke tradisi yang lebih luas yang disintesisnya. Peran karya ini dalam difusi global usul fikih Syafi'i telah menarik perhatian khusus dari para ulama yang tertarik pada penyebaran keilmuan Islam melalui jaringan perdagangan dan pendidikan. Kajian-kajian tentang kurikulum pesantren dan keilmuan Islam Melayu secara rutin membahas Jam al-Jawami sebagai teks fondasi.
content_ar: The Jam' al-Jawami' became one of the most widely taught texts in usul al-الفقه throughout the الشافعي world from the fifteenth century onward. Its adoption was facilitated by the prestige of the commentary associated with al-Mahalli and السيوطي, which provided the expansion and context needed to make the dense matan accessible to students. This matan-sharh (base text plus commentary) combination became the standard vehicle for teaching usul al-الفقه in الشافعي madrasas from Cairo to Southeast Asia. The influence of the Jam' al-Jawami' extended far beyond the Arab world. As الإسلام spread into Southeast Asia, الشافعي scholarship traveled with it, and the Jam' al-Jawami' with السيوطي's commentary became a foundational text in pesantren (traditional الإسلامي boarding schools) in Java, in pondok schools throughout the Malay world, and in الإسلامي educational institutions from West Africa to Central Asia. The work's reach gives it a global significance that few other usul texts can match. Numerous supercommentaries (hashiyah) were written on the commentary of السيوطي, further extending the pedagogical tradition attached to the text. These hashiyahs provided additional explanation, addressed objections, and related the discussions to contemporary legal questions, keeping the text actively relevant across generations. The layer upon layer of commentary attached to the Jam' al-Jawami' illustrates how the classical الإسلامي educational system نقل learning through the vehicle of a single authoritative text. Modern الإسلامي educational institutions in Egypt, the Levant, Southeast Asia, and elsewhere continue to teach the Jam' al-Jawami' with its commentaries as part of their usul al-الفقه curriculum. The work's conciseness makes it suitable for integration into curricula that also include other disciplines, while its comprehensiveness ensures that students who study it have been introduced to all the essential topics of the field. Academic العلماء have studied the Jam' al-Jawami' both as a text in its own right and as a window into the broader tradition it synthesizes. The work's role in the global diffusion of الشافعي usul al-الفقه has attracted particular attention from العلماء interested in the spread of الإسلامي learning through trade and educational networks. Studies of pesantren curriculum and Malay الإسلامي scholarship regularly discuss the Jam' al-Jawami' as a foundational text.
